truth.papers.A34.title: Dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength FeeMneAleC steel
truth.papers.A34.paper_scope: Study hot deformation behavior, microstructure evolution and dynamic recrystallization of low-density Fe-Mn-Al-C steel; establish DRX state diagram and grain size prediction model.

prediction.papers.A34.title: Dynamic recrystallization behavior and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel
prediction.papers.A34.paper_scope: Experimental study of hot deformation behavior, dynamic recrystallization kinetics, and microstructure evolution of low-density high-strength Fe-Mn-Al-C steel using Gleeble-3500 thermo-mechanical simulator, including activation energy, Zener-Hollomon parameter modeling, DRX state diagram, and grain size prediction model.

truth.processes.proc_fe25mn10al1_46c_hotcomp.d: Isothermal constant strain rate compression at temperatures 900–1150°C and strain rates 0.01–10 s^{-1}, with 60% reduction (true strain 0.92). Heating to 1200°C at 10°C/s, holding for 120 s, cooling to test temperature at 5°C/s, holding for 15 s, then compression and water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_fe25mn10al1_46c_hotcomp.p: Friction reduced by mica foils with high-temperature lubricants. Cylindrical samples, diameter 8 mm, height 12 mm.

prediction.processes.proc_fe25mn10al1_46c_hotcomp.d: Isothermal constant strain rate hot compression on Gleeble-3500 thermo-mechanical simulator at 900-1150°C and 0.01-10 s⁻¹ to 60% height reduction, following heating to 1200°C, homogenization, controlled cooling to test temperature, holding, and post-compression water quenching.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_900_0_01.process: Sprayed with water immediately after compression to cool the sample.

truth.structures.sample_900_0_01.overall_struc: Partially recrystallized, fine DRX grains along grain boundaries.
truth.structures.sample_900_0_01.microstructur: equiaxed DRX grains + deformed original grains

prediction.structures.sample_900_0_01.overall_struc: Polycrystalline microstructure exhibiting dynamic recrystallization behavior; deformation induces heterogeneous microstructure across difficult, small, and large deformation zones.
prediction.structures.sample_900_0_01.microstructur: DRX grains distributed along elongated original grain boundaries as small equiaxed necklace structures; grain boundaries serrated in large deformation zone
Fine and uniform at 950-1000 °C / 0.01-0.1 s⁻¹ and 1050-1100 °C / 1-10 s⁻¹; coarse at 1150 °C / 0.01 s⁻¹ exceeding 70 μm; heterogeneous with insufficient DRX at low temperature and high strain rate

prediction.properties.sample_900_0_01.mechanical.te: Rheological stress curves under hot compression at 900-1150 °C and 0.01-10 s⁻¹ exhibit peak stress behavior characteristic of dynamic recrystallization, with flow stress decreasing as temperature increases or strain rate decreases.

truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Isothermal constant strain rate compression tests
truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Flow stress curves obtained, peak stress sensitive to temperature and strain rate; DRX activation energy calculated.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_1.key_findi: DRX grain size and morphology determined; etching with 30% nitric acid alcohol.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_900_0_01: Isothermal constant strain rate thermal compression experiments and rheological stress measurement
Obtained rheological stress curves under 900-1150 °C and 0.01-10 s⁻¹; confirmed reproducibility; material is negative temperature-sensitive and positive strain rate-sensitive.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Correlation coefficient R=0.996, mean relative error MRE=4.53%
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: DRX average grain size prediction model
Levenberg-Marquardt optimization used; friction factor 0.3, convection coefficient 0.02 N/(s·mm·°C)
truth.computational_details.idx_1.analytical_m: Arrhenius hyperbolic sine equation for DRX
Q_p = 669.844 kJ/mol, α=0.00599, n=5.135 (average slope), etc.
Used to derive Zener-Hollomon parameter and characteristic strain/stress relationships.

prediction.computational_details.sample_900_0_01.va: Grain size prediction model validated against experimental data with correlation coefficient R = 0.996 and mean relative error MRE = 4.53%; absolute error mostly within 3 μm.
prediction.computational_details.sample_900_0_01.an: Constitutive relationship between peak stress, temperature, and strain rate for DRX activation energy calculation
α = 0.00599, Q_p = 669.844 kJ/mol, R = 8.314 J/(mol·K); average slopes k1 = 5.135 and k2 = 15.690 from linear fitting
Used to calculate dynamic recrystallization activation energy and Zener-Hollomon parameter Z.
Determination of critical strain for dynamic recrystallization
Critical strain identified via inflection point on work hardening rate curves; result consistent with literature for low-density high-strength steel.
DRX average grain size prediction model
Predict dynamic recrystallization average grain size under hot deformation conditions
Derived using Levenberg-Marquardt optimization; initial grain size and strain treated as fixed values; friction coefficient 0.3 and convection coefficient 0.02 N/(s·mm·°C) used in DEFORM-3D setup.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The critical strain ratio εc/εp ≈ 0.452 was determined using the Cingara-McQueen method and Poliak-Jonas criterion.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Peak stress at 0.01 s^{-1} decreased from 305.4 MPa (900°C) to 53.8 MPa (1150°C), a reduction of 82%; at 10 s^{-1} the reduction was 73% over the same temperature range.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Z parameter values (lnZ) for all conditions are given in Table 1, e.g., at 900°C/0.01 s^{-1} lnZ=64.08, at 1150°C/10 s^{-1} lnZ=58.92.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Optimal hot working windows for fine and uniform grains: 950–1000°C/0.01–0.1 s^{-1} and 1050–1100°C/1–10 s^{-1}.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The initial microstructure (Fig. 1) showed austenite grains; after compression, deformation zones (Fig. 8) exhibited different DRX degrees (zone 3 > zone 2 > zone 1).
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: DRX grain size exceeded 70 μm at 1150°C/0.01 s^{-1}, indicating rapid grain growth.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Flow stress curves in Fig. 3 show typical DRX characteristics with stress peaks.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The steel is a negative temperature-sensitive and positive strain rate-sensitive material.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Reproducibility of experimental phenomena was confirmed through repeated tests on same batch.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The dynamic recrystallization activation energy of Fe-Mn-Al-C steel is calculated as 669.844 kJ mol⁻¹ based on the Arrhenius hyperbolic sine constitutive equation and linear fitting of lnε_dot-ln[sinh(ασp)] and ln[sinh(ασp)]-1000/T plots.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: At strain rate 0.01 s⁻¹, peak stress decreased from 305.4 MPa at 900 °C to 53.8 MPa at 1150 °C, a reduction of 82%; at 10 s⁻¹, peak stress decreased by 73% over the same temperature range, indicating that peak stress is more sensitive to strain rate than to temperature within the tested ranges.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Zener-Hollomon parameter Z increases with increasing strain rate or decreasing temperature; calculated lnZ values range from 52.01 (1150 °C / 0.01 s⁻¹) to 70.99 (900 °C / 10 s⁻¹).
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Functional relationships between characteristic rheological stress values and lnZ were established by multiple linear regression: εc = 0.0093 lnZ - 0.43; σc = 1.0048(lnZ)² - 97.23 lnZ + 2379.08; εp = 0.020 lnZ - 0.94; σp = 0.98(lnZ)² - 94.21 lnZ + 2291.34; εs = 0.034 lnZ - 1.53; σs = 0.55(lnZ)² - 46.96 lnZ + 984.67.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Dynamic recrystallization state diagram (Fig. 7) identifies zone A (work hardening), zone B (partial DRX), and zone C (complete DRX); with increasing Z parameter, the initiation and completion of DRX become more difficult.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Microstructure after compression shows three deformation zones: 1-difficult deformation zone, 2-small deformation zone, 3-large deformation zone; DRX degree follows zone 3 > zone 2 > zone 1.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: DRX average grain size data (μm) measured from the large deformation zone core (Fig. 10) under different temperatures and strain rates: 900 °C: 12, 8, 6, 4; 950 °C: 18, 12, 8, 6; 1000 °C: 26, 18, 12, 8; 1050 °C: 34, 28, 16, 10; 1100 °C: 50, 38, 22, 14; 1150 °C: 75, 54, 32, 22 (for 0.01, 0.1, 1, 10 s⁻¹ respectively). At 1150 °C / 0.01 s⁻¹ grain size exceeds 70 μm and the structure is coarse; at low temperature and high strain rate, grains are small but DRX is insufficient and original large grains remain, leading to poor homogeneity.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Fine and uniform grain structure can be obtained by hot working in the range of 950-1000 °C / 0.01-0.1 s⁻¹ and 1050-1100 °C / 1-10 s⁻¹, where full DRX occurs with relatively fine and uniform grains.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The steel is a negative temperature-sensitive and positive strain rate-sensitive material; decreasing strain rate or increasing temperature reduces rheological stress by promoting DRX and dynamic recovery.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Post-compression water quenching preserves the high-temperature deformed microstructure for metallographic analysis; samples were etched with 30% nitric acid alcohol solution (volume fraction).
